commentary: Twelve activities this week versus nine last week, and honestly the body keeping up with that kind of volume is its own kind of flex. The Saturday run went up from 13.1 to 13.5 kilometres and the heart rate came down from 159 to 149, which means he's getting faster and easier at the same time — that's not effort, that's fitness, and it looks very good on him. The Balmoral double on Friday is giving chaotic energy in the best possible way, two open water swims back to back before a weights session, which is either brilliant periodisation or just the behaviour of someone who is absolutely feral about being in the ocean in a pair of budgie smugglers, and either way Himbo approves completely. Recovery looks solid across the board — nothing spiking where it shouldn't — so the only note is to actually use one of those rest days as a rest day next week, because even the hottest guys at the pool need to let the muscles catch up before they start showing off again.
metrics_commentary: Okay first of all, 7617 metres in the pool is unhinged in the best possible way — that body is built for water and it knows it. The single run session of 13.5 km is solid, though one session means we're leaving something on the table there, and honestly the legs deserve more chances to look good in motion. HRV at 47 with a seven-day average sitting at 51 tells me the body is working through something — not alarmed, just noting that it wants a little tenderness right now, maybe an extra sleep or two where you stretch out like you own the whole bed. And a VO2 Max of 57.3 is genuinely impressive, the kind of number that means you can keep going long after everyone else has sat down, which, honestly, very on-brand.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs across five lifts, and a weekly volume that climbed from 23,660 kg to 24,518 kg — he hit the target and then just kept going. The leg press jumping from 154 to 168 kg is the kind of number that makes a person feel the weight of themselves differently, and the seated cable row going from 77 to 84 kg means that back is getting genuinely, visibly wide. Hammer curls up from 16.8 to 19.6 kg, Bulgarian split squats up from 21.3 to 24 kg — these are the legs and arms of someone who is becoming something, and it shows. Full week, full effort, no gaps to be honest about, which honestly is almost annoying because it leaves nothing to tease him about.
